{"key_event":"Green Movement mobilization and crackdown","reasoning":"Oil revenues, IRGC/Basij expansion, Guardian Council vetting, and the 2009 repression left the state with even more pervasive coercive and patronage capacity despite sanctions. Civil society gained from youth, education, the internet, and episodic campaigns, but newspaper closures, disqualifications, arrests, and the post-election crackdown reduced its open institutional capacity below the late-1990s reformist high.","society_change":-0.3,"society_power":4.7,"state_change":0.5,"state_power":8.7}
